animal housing producing Eggs, hen, in shell (number) in Thailand. Additional description: CB8-Parent farm 5. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system boundaries include all relevant material and energy inputs and emissions from the cradle-to-customer supply chains of Canadian egg and egg products in 2012.* The system includes:+ Breeder Flock Facilities (chicks, feed, water, energy, manure management, spent hen disposal)+ Layer Facilities (housing system type and characteristics, management practices, on-farm resource use, hen performance)+ Transport (inputs and emissions from transport of inputs and outputs)+ Grading Facilities (grading, washing, packaging)+ Retail (distribution to retailers)+ Egg Breaking and Further Processing Facilities (production of liquid whole egg, egg white, or egg yolk products)**Functional Unit (FU):*** The FU is 1 kg of eggs leaving the farm gate.**Subsystems:*** Concentrate and Litter Production* Rearing Hen Husbandry (including hatching)* Laying Hen Husbandry* Transport**Ecological Indicators:*** Global Warming Potential (GWP)* Energy Use (EU)* Land Occupation (LO)* Fossil Phosphorus Use

animal housing producing Layer chicken, pullet in Mexico. Additional description: Chick breeding (6 weeks). What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of this production system includes:1. Animal housing (chicken farm)2. Feed production (including feed ingredients and production processes)3. Vaccination and cleaning processes4. Fattening and growth of chickens5. Slaughterhouse processes (including killing, evisceration, and packaging)6. Packaging of chickens7. Transportation of chickens from the farm to the slaughterhouse8. Water and energy use for cleaning and steam production9. Wastewater treatment in the WWTP10. Production of bedding materials (e.g. sawdust, wood shavings)11. Production of packaging materials (e.g. polystyrene trays, LDPE film)12. Fungicides and fertilizers used in the production systemThe following processes are excluded from the system boundary:1. Production of capital goods and infrastructures2. Animal drugs and the production of one-day-old chicks entering the breeder farms3. Composting of manure (although it is used as a fertilizer)4. Transportation of manure from the farms to the composting site (although it is used as a fertilizer)

permanent pasture producing Milk, cow, raw in United Kingdom. producing Broiler chicken in Russia. Additional description: Poultry production under the conditions of the central region in european Russia. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the life cycle assessment (LCA) relevant information for the broiler chicken production system:**System Boundaries:*** Chicken farm* Slaughterhouse* Feed production line (including water treatment and steam production)* Packaging process (including packaging materials production and distribution)* Transportation of chickens from farm to slaughterhouse**Excluded Processes:*** Production of cleaning agents (detergents and disinfectants)* Production of vaccines and antibiotics* Solid-stream processing (activities related to processing of organic residues)* Production of capital goods and infrastructures**Included Processes:*** Reception of chicks at the farm gate* Fattening period of 34 days* Production of chicken feed (maize, wheat, soybean cake, soybean oil, monocalcium phosphate, protein concentrate, and fats)* Vaccination and antibiotic administration* Cleaning activities at the chicken farm* Wastewater treatment in a small WWTP on the chicken farm* Slaughterhouse activities (killing, scalding, evisceration, and packaging)* Transportation of chickens from farm to slaughterhouse

animal housing producing Layer chicken, pullet in Mexico. Additional description: Bird development phase (10 weeks). What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: Here is a summary of the relevant life cycle assessment (LCA) information:**System Boundaries:*** The system includes all activities from the chicken farm to the slaughterhouse, including feed production, animal rearing, and packaging.* The system excludes activities outside the slaughterhouse, such as solid-stream processing and the production of capital goods and infrastructures.* The system boundaries include:+ Chicken farm: production of feed, animal rearing, and production of bedding materials.+ Slaughterhouse: production of broilers, processing, and packaging.+ Feed production: production of animal feed, including water treatment and wastewater management.+ Packaging: production of packaging materials and their distribution to the slaughterhouse.**Excluded Activities:*** Solid-stream processing (e.g. processing of feathers, heads, and viscera).* Production of capital goods and infrastructures (e.g. machinery, buildings).* Animal drugs and the production of one-day-old chicks entering the breeder farms.* Composting of manure (although some manure is utilized as fertilizer).**Functional Unit:*** 1 kg Carcass Weight (CW) is used as the functional unit for the LCA assessment.**Inputs and Emissions:***

animal housing producing Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) in Spain. Additional description: Pasture-based milk cycle. What is in the system boundary? Additional Context: The system boundary of the animal housing producing milk production system in Spain includes:* Farm activities related to milk production* On-farm and off-farm feeds production* Water and energy resources* Purchase of feeds (concentrates, fodders, silages, cereals, proteic feeds)* Purchase of bedding materials (straw, corn stalks, sawdust)* Production of manure from animal breeding* Production of ancillary inputs such as fuels and electricity* Drinking water for the cattle* Farm infrastructure (roads, irrigation infrastructure, buildings, tools)* Non-local feeds with related production and transport processes* Carbon sequestration and biogenic carbon emissions of locally produced feeds (alfalfa and maize) are not considered due to vegetation cycles shorter than one year.The system does not include:* Equipment of the farms with specialized tools* Medicines and seeds* Emissions from land use change for the production of on-farm feeds* Emissions from land use change for purchased feed production* Emissions from land use change for soybeans in Brazil* Emissions from animal respiration and CO2 fixed by plants through photosynthesis* Employee transport and corporate office operation* Manure leaving the farm without
